content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me examine atom C:9 (carbon with index 9) specifically.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:9 is connected to:
- C:10 (carbon, index 10) - bonded directly
- C:8 (carbon, index 8) - bonded directly  
- H:12 (hydrogen, index 12) - bonded directly

The bonds are shown as yellow rods connecting these atoms. C:9 appears to be part of a ring structure and has exactly three covalent bonds: two to carbon atoms (C:8 and C:10) and one to a hydrogen atom (H:12).

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 9325, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_9325.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 9 (C:9) are:
- Atom index 8, element C
- Atom index 10, element C  
- Atom index 12, element H

Geomconf_index: 9325

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
